attributes.magic: PE32 executable (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

signature_matches.name: resolve function by parsing PE exports
signature_matches.description: load-code/pe
signature_matches.rule_src:
rule:
  meta:
    name: resolve function by parsing PE exports
    namespace: load-code/pe
    authors:
      - sara-rn
    scopes:
      static: function
      dynamic: unsupported  # requires characteristic, offset, mnemonic features
    examples:
      - 73CE04892E5F39EC82B00C02FC04C70F:0x406BA1
  features:
    - and:
      - os: windows
      - or:
        - characteristic: loop
        - mnemonic: movzx
      - and:
        - offset: 0x3C = IMAGE_DOS_HEADER.PE.e_lfanew
        - or:
          - and:
            - arch: i386
            - offset: 0x78 = offset to IMAGE_DATA_DIRECTORY[IMAGE_DIRECTORY_ENTRY_EXPORT]
          - and:
            - arch: amd64
            - offset: 0x88 = offset to IMAGE_DATA_DIRECTORY[IMAGE_DIRECTORY_ENTRY_EXPORT]
        - 3 or more:
          - offset: 0x14 = IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY.NumberOfFunctions
          - offset: 0x24 = IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY.AddressOfNameOrdinals
          - offset: 0x20 = IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY.AddressOfNames
          - offset: 0x18 = IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY.NumberOfNames
          - offset: 0x1C = IMAGE_EXPORT_DIRECTORY.AddressOfFunctions
      - optional:
        - or:
          - api: LoadLibrary
          - api: strcmp

signature_matches.name: encode data using XOR
signature_matches.description: data-manipulation/encoding/xor
signature_matches.rule_src:
rule:
  meta:
    name: encode data using XOR
    namespace: data-manipulation/encoding/xor
    authors:
      - moritz.raabe@mandiant.com
    scopes:
      static: basic block
      dynamic: unsupported  # requires characteristic, Not features
    att&ck:
      - Defense Evasion::Obfuscated Files or Information [T1027]
    mbc:
      - Defense Evasion::Obfuscated Files or Information::Encoding-Standard Algorithm [E1027.m02]
      - Data::Encode Data::XOR [C0026.002]
    examples:
      - 2D3EDC218A90F03089CC01715A9F047F:0x403D7E
  features:
    - and:
      - characteristic: tight loop
      - characteristic: nzxor
      - not:
        - description: filter for potential false positives
        - or:
          - or:
            - description: unsigned bitwise negation operation (~i)
            - number: 0xFFFFFFFF = bitwise negation for unsigned 32 bits
            - number: 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF = bitwise negation for unsigned 64 bits
          - or:
            - description: signed bitwise negation operation (~i)
            - number: 0x0FFFFFFF = bitwise negation for signed 32 bits
            - number: 0x0FFFFFFFFFFFFFFF = bitwise negation for signed 64 bits
          - or:
            - description: Magic constants used in the implementation of strings functions.
            # such as `strlen` and `strcat` in the Windows standard library:
            # (((i - 0x81010101) ^ ~i) & 0x81010100)
            ## 32 bits
            - number: 0x7EFEFEFF = optimized string constant for 32 bits
            - number: 0x81010101 = -0x81010101 = 0x7EFEFEFF
            - number: 0x81010100 = 0x81010100 = ~0x7EFEFEFF
            ## 64 bits
            - number: 0x7EFEFEFEFEFEFEFF = optimized string constant for 64 bits
            - number: 0x8101010101010101 = -0x8101010101010101 = 0x7EFEFEFEFEFEFEFF
            - number: 0x8101010101010100 = 0x8101010101010100 = ~0x7EFEFEFEFEFEFEFF

signature_matches.name: accept command line arguments
signature_matches.description: host-interaction/cli
signature_matches.rule_src:
rule:
  meta:
    name: accept command line arguments
    namespace: host-interaction/cli
    authors:
      - moritz.raabe@mandiant.com
      - anushka.virgaonkar@mandiant.com
    scopes:
      static: function
      dynamic: call
    att&ck:
      - Execution::Command and Scripting Interpreter [T1059]
    mbc:
      - Execution::Command and Scripting Interpreter [E1059]
    examples:
      - e5369ac309f1be6d77afeeb3edab0ed8:0x402760
  features:
    - or:
      - api: GetCommandLine
      - api: CommandLineToArgv
      - api: System.Environment::GetCommandLineArgs

signature_matches.name: encrypt data using RC4 PRGA
signature_matches.description: data-manipulation/encryption/rc4
signature_matches.rule_src:
rule:
  meta:
    name: encrypt data using RC4 PRGA
    namespace: data-manipulation/encryption/rc4
    authors:
      - moritz.raabe@mandiant.com
    scopes:
      static: function
      dynamic: unsupported  # requires characteristic, mnemonic, basicblock features
    att&ck:
      - Defense Evasion::Obfuscated Files or Information [T1027]
    mbc:
      - Cryptography::Encrypt Data::RC4 [C0027.009]
      - Cryptography::Generate Pseudo-random Sequence::RC4 PRGA [C0021.004]
    examples:
      - 34404A3FB9804977C6AB86CB991FB130:0x403DB0
      - 34404A3FB9804977C6AB86CB991FB130:0x403E50
      - 9324D1A8AE37A36AE560C37448C9705A:0x4049F0
      - 73CE04892E5F39EC82B00C02FC04C70F:0x4064C6
  features:
    - and:
      # TODO: maybe add characteristic for nzxor reg size
      - count(characteristic(nzxor)): 1
      - or:
        - match: calculate modulo 256 via x86 assembly
        # compiler may do this via zero-extended mov from 8-bit register
        - count(mnemonic(movzx)): 4 or more
      # should not call (many) functions
      - count(characteristic(calls from)): (0, 4)
      # should not be too simple or too complex (50 is picked by intuition)
      - count(basic blocks): (4, 50)
      - match: contain loop
      - optional:
        - or:
          - number: 0xFF
          - number: 0x100

mitre_attack_techniques.signature_description: encode data using XOR
accept command line arguments
encrypt data using RC4 PRGA

signature_matches.name: stealth_network
signature_matches.description: Network activity detected but not expressed in monitor API logs

signature_matches.name: queries_locale_api
signature_matches.description: Queries the computer locale (possible geofencing)

signature_matches.name: stealth_timeout
signature_matches.description: Possible date expiration check, exits too soon after checking local time

signature_matches.name: language_check_registry
signature_matches.description: Checks system language via registry key (possible geofencing)

signature_matches.name: encrypted_ioc
signature_matches.description: At least one IP Address, Domain, or File Name was found in a crypto call

signature_matches.name: resumethread_remote_process
signature_matches.description: Resumed a thread in another process
match_data: {"thread_resumed": "Process mlserver.exe with process ID 6984 resumed a thread in another process with the process ID 5176"}
{"thread_resumed": "Process werfault.exe with process ID 5176 resumed a thread in another process with the process ID 6984"}

signature_matches.name: createtoolhelp32snapshot_module_enumeration
signature_matches.description: Enumerates the modules from a process (may be used to locate base addresses in process injection)

signature_matches.name: network_http
signature_matches.description: Performs some HTTP requests

signature_matches.name: packer_entropy
signature_matches.description: The binary likely contains encrypted or compressed data

signature_matches.name: contains_pe_overlay
signature_matches.description: The PE file contains an overlay
match_data: {"overlay": "Contains overlay at offset 0x00009a00 with size: 1536 bytes"}

signature_matches.name: injection_rwx
signature_matches.description: Creates RWX memory

signature_matches.name: antivm_generic_system
signature_matches.description: Checks the system manufacturer, likely for anti-virtualization

signature_matches.name: suspicious_iocontrol_codes
signature_matches.description: Uses suspicious IO control codes

signature_matches.name: procmem_yara
signature_matches.description: Yara detections observed in process dumps, payloads or dropped files
match_data: {"Hit": "PID 5176 triggered the Yara rule 'shellcode_get_eip' with data '['{ E8 00 00 00 00 58 }']'"}
{"Hit": "PID 6984 triggered the Yara rule 'shellcode_get_eip' with data '['{ E8 00 00 00 00 5A }', '{ E8 00 00 00 00 58 }']'"}

signature_matches.name: static_pe_anomaly
signature_matches.description: Anomalous binary characteristics
match_data: {"anomaly": "Minimum OS version is older than NT4 yet the PE timestamp year is newer than 2000"}
{"anomaly": "Entrypoint of binary is located outside of any mapped sections"}

signature_matches.name: antivm_generic_disk
signature_matches.description: Queries information on disks, possibly for anti-virtualization

mitre_attack_techniques.signature_description: Adversaries may use bootkits to persist on systems.
Adversaries may inject code into processes in order to evade process-based defenses as well as possibly elevate privileges.
Adversaries may modify file time attributes to hide new or changes to existing files.
Adversaries may delete or modify artifacts generated within systems to remove evidence of their presence or hinder defenses.
Adversaries may employ various means to detect and avoid virtualization and analysis environments.
Adversaries may attempt to make an executable or file difficult to discover or analyze by encrypting, encoding, or otherwise obfuscating its contents on the system or in transit.
Adversaries may perform software packing or virtual machine software protection to conceal their code.
An adversary may attempt to get detailed information about the operating system and hardware, including version, patches, hotfixes, service packs, and architecture.
Adversaries may attempt to get information about running processes on a system.
Adversaries may interact with the Windows Registry to gather information about the system, configuration, and installed software.
Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection/network filtering by blending in with existing traffic.

signature_matches.description: Uses 32bit PE files
match_data: RELOCS_STRIPPED, EXECUTABLE_IMAGE, LINE_NUMS_STRIPPED, LOCAL_SYMS_STRIPPED, BYTES_REVERSED_LO, 32BIT_MACHINE, BYTES_REVERSED_HI

May try to detect the virtual machine to hinder analysis (VM artifact strings found in memory)
Creates mutexes
PE file has an executable .text section which is very likely to contain packed code (zlib compression ratio < 0.3)
Creates a process in suspended mode (likely to inject code)
Checks if the current process is being debugged
Binary may include packed or encrypted code
URLs found in memory or binary data
Sample crashes during execution, try analyze it on another analysis machine
